rows: `Provider.Capability()` returns C3 `ProviderCapability`; capability changes belong to C3.
Adapter draft `Type` must be registered in the event catalog; append authority stays separated.
Adapter may fill only allowed draft fields; ingest finalizes required event fields.
Adapter does not decide transitions; ingest/orchestrator interprets run signals.
One provider instance, one lease, and one `(RuntimeID, CapabilityFingerprint)` pair.
Adapter reads prepared workdir/native config; C6 creates workdirs first.
`ExposesUnsafePermissionBypass` is a risk signal; C7 policy decides activation.
G5 pre-execute handshake calls adapter initialize/firstline probes before lease activation.
Runtime identity, capability drift, and reserved upgrade evidence are delegated there.
